title: Formation Flight link-sampling density versus direction subdecomposition
scientificQuestion: Within the boundary-source census's dominant c16-to-c20 link-sampling term, is the discrepancy generated primarily by total areal boundary-link density or by redistribution among D3Q19 directions?
secondaryProbes: the other owner at follower phase 0.035 and both owners at follower phase 0.095; context only, never substitutes for the primary decision

exactSubdecomposition.conditionalMomentumExchangeMean: m_r(q) is the already audited raw-reflected-plus-reconstructed-incoming population sum divided by link count
exactSubdecomposition.identity: L(q) = L_D(q) + L_p(q) exactly for every direction

decisionRule.arealLinkDensityDominated: density-term weighted-L1 share is at least 0.6 at the primary probe
decisionRule.directionRedistributionDominated: direction-redistribution weighted-L1 share is at least 0.6 at the primary probe
decisionRule.nextIfArealLinkDensityDominated: run a geometry-only subcell-offset ensemble at the two locked phases to distinguish lattice-phase aliasing from a systematic curved-surface link-density bias before changing fluid physics
decisionRule.nextIfDirectionRedistributionDominated: run a source-locked fixed-density direction cross-application on the dominant D3Q19 pairs before changing interpolation or collision
decisionRule.nextIfMixedDensityAndDirection: run one geometry-only c18 bridge at the primary phase and retain both geometric pathways

claimBoundary: This archive-only identity can select the next geometry diagnostic. It cannot authorize a production geometry/boundary change, a quantitative formation benefit, or a biological claim.
